objective.problem: Decide whether individually weak IdrEvent signals form a credible campaign when their order and infrastructure relationships are evaluated together.
objective.decision_target: Advisory IntelligenceFinding (campaign probability + ranked entities + evidence event IDs) consumed by idr-sentinel deterministic corroboration.
success_metrics: Held-out ROC-AUC / PR-AUC / Brier under chronological 60/20/20 splits
S6+GNN hybrid must beat or match every ablation variant on Brier under the same split
Every finding carries evidence_event_ids resolvable to source telemetry

constraints: Advisory boundary: model output must never trigger PanicResponse; idr-sentinel corroborates against primary telemetry first
Latent state is never an auditable substitute for source events; evidence IDs are mandatory in findings
Chronological splits only; no training on future information relative to prediction time
Synthetic benchmark results must never be presented as production detection accuracy
python >= 3.11; deps: numpy, torch >= 2.2, scikit-learn; GPL-3.0-or-later

sources: NDJSON streams of idr_common::IdrEvent from idr-main; synthetic matched campaigns from simulator.py
schemas: IdrEvent: {id, timestamp, source, severity, kind{type,...}, metadata} — validated in schema.py
provenance: Simulator seeds are deterministic; event IDs are seed-derived UUIDs

outputs: IntelligenceFinding JSON (pipeline.py): campaign_id, escalation_probability (affine-calibrated), raw_escalation_probability, calibration, predicted_next_stage (ATT&CK), observed_attack_stages, related_entities, evidence_event_ids, model_version, graph_nodes, graph_relations, engine_version, feature_schema_hash, scored_at, feature_drift, continues_campaign, windows_observed; entity_evidence (per-entity why: occlusion features, typed edges, ATT&CK); applied_suppressions
Ablation benchmark report (training.py, reports/demo.json) with per-scenario generalization and rolling-origin CV verdict
Checkpoint artifacts/hybrid_model.pt (dict: state_dict incl. temperature+cal_bias buffers, dims, use_s6/use_gnn/pooling, manifest, feature_stats)

components.role: entity extraction (host/process/hash/ip/prefix/asn/domain/device + user/session/cloud identity), typed edges, 22-dim feature vector
temporal graph: per-entity histories, per-entity deltas, time-decayed edges, node budget, typed edges, evidence provenance
SelectiveSSM (step() cell + hoisted forward), ResidualGraphLayer, GatedAttentionPool, CampaignModel, checkpoint save/load
11 scenario families incl. stale_preamble & timing_only (temporal-physics isolation tests); per-event scenario + stage_index ground truth
EngineConfig: typed single home for tunables + prior tables + config hash + TOML loader
deterministic kind->ATT&CK tactic/technique table, observed stages, next-stage prediction
frozen-manifest benchmark runner with regression floors (benchmarks/v1.json)
GraphBudget + audited LRU EvictionRecord for bounded node memory (W15 streaming infra)
structured per-entity evidence (occlusion attribution, typed edges, ATT&CK) + suppression allowlist
cross-window campaign identity: durable-entity fingerprints, weighted-Jaccard matcher, content-addressed IDs, JSON-persisted CampaignRegistry

interfaces.name: IdrEvent
interfaces.contract: canonical tagged shape from idr_common; kind must contain 'type'

interfaces.name: checkpoint
interfaces.contract: state_dict (incl. temperature + cal_bias buffers), feature_dim/hidden_dim/state_dim, use_s6/use_gnn/pooling, manifest (engine_version, feature_schema_hash, config_hash, created_at, calibration string), feature_stats; loader defaults missing temperature/cal_bias to identity and raises SchemaMismatchError on feature-schema drift; raw v1 state dicts still load; also carries time_mode (global/per_entity/time_aware); time-aware S6 adds a time_weight param (absent otherwise); also carries decay_half_life (None = binary edges)

interfaces.name: TemporalGraph
interfaces.contract: node_ids, sequences [N,steps,22], mask, deltas [N,steps] (per-entity log1p gaps), symmetric degree-normalized adjacency, evidence_ids per node, relation_counts, typed_edges (node,node,relation), evictions (audited node-budget drops)

interfaces.name: IntelligenceFinding
interfaces.contract: v6 fields + continues_campaign (bool) and windows_observed (int), populated when score_events receives a CampaignRegistry; without one they default to False/1 and campaign_id stays the per-call first-event derivation — additive, back-compat

interfaces.name: CampaignRegistry
interfaces.contract: JSON {records: [{campaign_id, fingerprint {entity: weight}, windows_observed, first_seen, last_seen}]}; match_or_register(entities, scored_at) -> (campaign_id, continues, windows): weighted Jaccard >= 0.5 over durable entities (process:/session: excluded; hash 3.0 > domain/user 2.5 > ip/cloud 2.0 > prefix/asn 1.5 > device 1.0 > host 0.5) continues and unions the best match, else registers a blake2s content-addressed id; empty fingerprint -> sentinel idr-campaign-unfingerprinted, never registered

interfaces.name: LabeledWindow
interfaces.contract: {window_id, label 0|1, events [IdrEvent,...]} one-per-line in *.labeled.ndjson; loader sorts by window start so the chronological split stays honest; single-class split segments fail loudly

decisions.decision: Advisory-only integration; findings feed idr-sentinel, never actuate responses
Explicit per-step S6 scan chosen for auditability over throughput in v0.1
Masked max-pools fill with -inf so isfinite guards genuinely zero all-padding rows (was -1e9 no-op)
Ablation variants construct only the submodules they use, keeping parameter counts honest
Checkpoints carry their own dimensions; CLI must not hardcode hyperparameters
campaign_id derives from chronologically first event, independent of caller ordering
Push CI restricted to main; PR commits build once via pull_request trigger
mypy follows the running interpreter version; pinning 3.11 breaks against numpy 2.5 PEP 695 stubs on newer Pythons

Gated MIL attention pooling replaces mean+max in the default model: attention scores are the entity ranking, so evidence selection finally receives training gradient (node_head previously had grad=None). uniform_pool retained as fifth ablation arm; uniform path stays numerically identical to legacy checkpoints

Design roadmap adopted: 4 phases / 14 workstreams synthesized from 24 judged proposals; docs/ROADMAP.md is canonical. Rejected: learned next-stage head (circular supervision), dense R-GCN typed adjacency (unfalsifiable pre-Phase-2)
Checkpoints persist ablation flags and pooling mode so every trained variant round-trips (verify-workflow finding); non-dict payloads rejected with a clear error

All tunables live in config.EngineConfig (TOML-loadable, hashable); prior tables moved to config with schema.py re-export; ENGINE_VERSION defined in config to avoid the __init__ import cycle. Checkpoints embed a ModelManifest and refuse to load across feature-schema changes

Temperature calibration fitted by validation-NLL grid search with T=1 always in the grid, making 'post-scaling validation NLL never worsens' a hard CI-assertable guarantee (the roadmap's ECE<=pre wording is not guaranteeable by NLL fitting). Findings report raw + calibrated probability + calibration string; ECE/log-loss/MCE added to ablation metrics

Dataset labels are seeded random draws with a deterministic retry guaranteeing both classes in every chronological segment (the former index%2 alternation leaked parity across split boundaries); _fit weights BCE by neg/pos, calibration stays unweighted so probabilities match true frequencies; _evaluate adds recall@FPR{1%,0.1%} and precision@5

Next-stage stays deterministic per the roadmap's rejection of the learned head: attack.py maps 11 of 12 kinds to ATT&CK tactic/technique (triage_classification is a correlator meta-event, deliberately unmapped); prediction advances past the furthest observed tactic, fixing the old presence-lookup's order blindness

Simulator scenarios are deterministic in (label, seed, scenario) with no RNG object; every scenario keeps the same six kill-chain kinds so nothing separates classes except convergence/order/semantics; train_ablation reports scenario_generalization for the trained hybrid across all families

benchmarks/v1.json freezes generator config + floors; scenario floors are Brier-only because ranking metrics observably flip (distractor ROC 0.0<->1.0) at CI budgets and would flake. CI runs the manifest every build. Checkpoints embed per-feature training histograms; scoring attaches advisory PSI drift (0.2 flag line) to findings

Real labeled windows enter via --data with zero model changes (windows_to_batch reuses build_temporal_graph); the report records data_source and the observed malicious rate; simulator diagnostics (evidence precision, scenario generalization) still run as synthetic sidecars

rolling_origin_ablation: expanding-window folds (40% minimum origin, 15% val/test blocks, folds<=3) x seed replicates; Brier is the decision metric (ranking saturates); best_model declared only when the paired margin exceeds the paired per-fold std, else 'tie'. First run confirms the roadmap's claim: 9-fold verdict is tie (margin 0.024 vs std 0.124) — single-split winners were noise

Calibration is affine (scale + bias), not temperature-only: adversarial verification proved a pure scale cannot undo the constant log(pos_weight) shift that class-weighted training introduces, so calibrated probabilities were inflated under imbalance. affine_calibration_params fits both by LBFGS from identity, bounded to scale in [0.2,5] and bias in [-5,5] to prevent separable-data runaway, accepted only if validation NLL does not worsen. Single calibration_label() method shared by checkpoint and finding. cal_bias buffer added; older checkpoints default it to 0

Identity entities: user: (global — the same actor links events across hosts, the load-bearing lateral-movement pivot), session:{host}:{sid}, cloud: nodes with authenticates_to/owns/spawns/accesses edges; has_user + has_identity_pivot features (FEATURE_DIM 20->22, schema hash rotates, checkpoints regenerate). lateral_movement scenario scatters all infrastructure so only the shared user connects the campaign; cross-host linkage is structural (global user node), not a per-event feature

W11 per-entity time: build_temporal_graph tracks last_seen per entity; under per_entity/time_aware the delta_seconds_log feature becomes the gap since THAT entity was last seen (feature values change but FEATURE_NAMES/schema-hash do not), and a deltas [N,steps] channel carries per-entity gaps to a time-aware SelectiveSSM (delta += time_weight*dt, time_weight init 0 = identity). HONEST RESULT: time_ablation on low_and_slow at 80/3 gives global Brier 0.221 vs per_entity/time_aware 0.238 — time-awareness does not beat the global gap at demo budget (time_weight barely moves in 3 epochs; per-entity resets lose graph-level timing signal). Capability shipped with a falsifiable test; verdict recorded, not assumed

W12 delivers the two FALSIFIABLE parts of the roadmap's bounded-decayed-sparse graph: time-decayed edge weights (0.5**(age/half_life), off by default) with a distractor ablation, and GraphBudget+EvictionRecord audited node eviction (unit-tested, never triggers at demo scale). DEFERRED to W15 (streaming): torch.sparse.mm (pure overhead at <=24 nodes) and the build_temporal_graph->replay-wrapper refactor (needs streaming as a second consumer) — shipping either now would be unfalsified complexity, per the roadmap's own R-GCN rejection logic. HONEST RESULT: decay_ablation on distractor gives none/1h/15m Brier 0.2366/0.2366/0.2364 — decay barely moves because distractor plants benign edges contemporaneously, not as a stale preamble; the mechanism is correct but this scenario does not stress it. decay_half_life defaults None (shipped hybrid unchanged; floors stable)

W14 structured evidence: the finding keeps its flat top-k for Rust back-compat but adds entity_evidence — per ranked entity, the events implicating it, typed edges to other ranked entities (graph.py now retains per-pair typed_edges, previously only counted), top-3 features by occlusion attribution (zero each of 22 channels, measure node-logit drop), and ATT&CK techniques from its events. All derived from the existing graph/model, so nothing bypasses the evidence boundary. apply_suppressions attenuates allowlisted entities (exact id or 'prefix:') out of the RANKING only — campaign probability untouched, finding never hidden from idr-sentinel, applied_suppressions audited
decisions.where: docs/ROADMAP.md W14 — chosen as best-ROI: completes the actionable-evidence product, dodges the synthetic-benchmark wall that stalled W11/W12

decisions.decision: Benchmark hardening to validate W11/W12 (user directive 'fix the benchmark first'): added stale_preamble (identical benign preamble ~2 days before the recent window) and timing_only (identical structure/content, ONLY inter-event timing differs) scenarios. FINDING: temporal physics is not empirically differentiated on synthetic data. On timing_only every mode separates at ROC/PR 1.0 and global wins Brier decisively (0.186 vs 0.210) — the scalar delta_seconds_log feature already encodes timing, so per-entity clock + time-aware S6 + edge decay add variance without signal. Documented in reports/AUDIT.md. Recommends flipping the shipped time_mode default from time_aware to global (simpler + better); deferred to keep this a pure benchmark/doc addition. Validating temporal physics needs real labeled campaigns (--data path), not more synthetic scenarios

Flipped the shipped CampaignModel default time_mode from time_aware to global (and build_temporal_graph default likewise), acting on ADR-25's decisive evidence that global >= time_aware on every synthetic scenario and the delta feature already captures timing. The simpler mode is now default; per_entity/time_aware stay opt-in for real-data deployments. decay_half_life already defaulted None. Benchmark floors re-verified green with the global hybrid

W13 S6 step() cell: SelectiveSSM refactored so a single _recurrence path is shared by forward (loops it with input_proj hoisted over the whole sequence) and a public step(x_t, state, output, active, delta_t) cell for streaming/export. initial_state() gives a fresh (state, output); snapshot/restore is lossless. Numerical-equivalence test asserts looping step() == forward for both time modes; forward output is bit-identical to the pre-refactor loop so benchmark floors are unchanged. This is the prerequisite for W15 streaming and W17 ONNX/Rust export (one code path, no train/serve skew). Heavy throughput work (associative scan, torch.compile) deferred per roadmap

W16 campaign identity is deterministic set-matching, not a model: durable-entity fingerprints (weighted by continuity strength — hash/domain/user outweigh host; ephemeral process:/session: excluded) matched by weighted Jaccard at 0.5. A match continues the campaign (fingerprint unioned so identity follows infrastructure as it grows), a miss registers a blake2s content-addressed id (order-independent, reproducible). The registry is opt-in (score_events(registry=...), CLI --registry PATH load->match->save); without it findings behave exactly as before. Sentinel id for windows with no durable entities. Auditable by construction — idr-sentinel can recompute every match decision from the finding + registry JSON

completed: v0.1.0 package merged to main (PR #2); CI lint fix (PR #5); Ocean Reef takeoff migrated out to LeeLupton/pricing (PR #4, pricing#1)
Review follow-ups + test hardening (4->14) + docstrings + mypy gate (PR #6)
state.json bootstrap (this slice)
Design roadmap synthesized and committed (docs/ROADMAP.md); state task graph now mirrors it

Adversarial diff verification of PR #6 (19 agents): 4 distinct upheld findings, all fixed in the W1 slice (variant round-trip, legacy-shape test, vacuous order-invariance test, roundtrip weight fidelity)
W1: attention-gated pooling + evidence_precision_at_5 metric + fifth ablation arm
W2: EngineConfig + ModelManifest (config.py, registry.py, configs/default.toml, provenance fields on findings, schema-hash rejection on load)
W3: temperature calibration (validation-NLL grid, T buffer in state_dict, manifest calibration string) + ECE/log-loss/MCE metrics + raw/calibrated finding fields
W4: seeded random labels + pos_weight + recall@FPR and precision@k operating-point metrics; CLI --malicious-rate
W5: ATT&CK mapping (attack.py) — Phase 1 complete
W6: graded + adversarial simulator scenarios with per-scenario generalization table
W7: versioned benchmark suite (benchmarks/v1.json, runner, CI gate) + feature-snapshot drift on findings
W8: LabeledWindow schema + dataio loader + --data training path
W9: rolling-origin CV + replicates with statistical best-model verdict (CLI: ablation)

Verification fixes (wk3kr3oza, 12 upheld): affine calibration replacing broken temperature-only [BLOCKING], degenerate recall@FPR handling, Rust sketch + state.json contract sync [BLOCKING], attack docstring, and 5 vacuous/tautological tests rewritten to fail on real regressions [2 BLOCKING]
W10: identity entities (user/session/cloud) + edges + features + lateral_movement scenario — Phase 2 complete
W11: per-entity time deltas + time-aware S6 discretization + time_ablation (honest verdict: global wins on low_and_slow at demo budget)
W12: time-decayed edges + GraphBudget audited eviction + decay_ablation (honest near-tie on distractor); sparse-mm and replay-wrapper deferred to W15
W14: structured per-entity evidence (occlusion attribution, typed edges, ATT&CK per entity) + analyst suppression allowlist

Benchmark hardening: stale_preamble + timing_only scenarios; VERIFIED temporal physics (W11/W12) undifferentiated on synthetic data (global beats time-aware on timing_only 0.186 vs 0.210); documented in AUDIT.md
Flip default time_mode time_aware->global (evidence-driven, ADR-26); benchmark floors green; default locked by test
v0.1 consolidation: README refreshed to match the shipped engine (capabilities, all CLI subcommands, full repo map, honest temporal-physics note, updated resume bullet)
W13: S6 step() cell (shared _recurrence, streaming step(), lossless snapshot/restore, numerical equivalence to forward) — enables W15/W17

W16: campaign-identity registry (campaigns.py; weighted durable-entity fingerprint matching, content-addressed IDs, opt-in via score_events(registry=)/CLI --registry; finding v7 adds continues_campaign + windows_observed)
active: W15 streaming scorer (streaming.py: EntityState + StreamingScorer over W13 step() + bounded_graph; sparse adjacency + replay-wrapper deferred from W12 land here)
blocked: W17 ONNX export + Rust bridge (uses W13 step(); NEEDS user machine — Rust toolchain + idr-main)

commands_run.cmd: ruff check src tests && python -m mypy src

commands_run.result: passed, 0 violations (registry is opt-in; scoring path unchanged without it)

tests: tests: 91 — adds W16 coverage: durable-vs-ephemeral fingerprinting, host-only insufficient / infrastructure sufficient matching, order-independent content addressing, cross-window continuity + new-campaign registration + sentinel, persistence round-trip, score_events registry stability, CLI --registry across invocations

benchmarks.roc_pr: all five variants saturate at 1.0 (synthetic separability; Phase 2 W6 exists to fix this)
benchmarks.note: ep@5 chance floor ~0.5 on 9-node graphs; 3 epochs is deliberately minimal — metric exists to make Phase 2 improvements measurable
all arms select T=0.1995 (grid edge) — demo models are systematically underconfident at 3 epochs; interior T expected on real training budgets

benchmarks.run: demo 80/3 seed 7, 2026-07-23, W6 scenario generalization (hybrid, v0_easy-trained)
benchmarks.note: first measurable cross-scenario differentiation; ranking metrics still saturate — W9 replicates and larger eval sets sharpen this
margin 0.024 < paired std 0.124 — the statistical machinery correctly refuses to crown a winner at this budget

assumptions: Simulator class separability makes ranking metrics uninformative at n<=80; only calibration (Brier/ECE) and cross-scenario Brier separate variants — W6 provides the harder families
predicted_next_stage / observed_attack_stages come from a deterministic ATT&CK table (attack.py), not the model — auditable by design
evidence_precision_at_5 uses multi-event entity membership as ground truth; valid for the current simulator convergence design only
uncertainty: No real labeled campaigns yet (W8 path exists); production accuracy unknown
Homogeneous message passing; typed variant deferred until split-host evasion can falsify it

VERIFIED via timing_only (identical structure, timing-only discriminator): temporal physics (W11/W12) add no measurable value on synthetic data because the scalar delta feature already captures timing; global time_mode beats time_aware. The machinery is architecturally correct but needs real multi-entity streams to prove out (--data path). See reports/AUDIT.md

artifacts.role: canonical 4-phase/14-workstream improvement roadmap with judge scores and rejections
Arch container CI: install, import check, pytest, ruff, mypy

latest_event.summary: W16 campaign-identity registry shipped: stable cross-window campaign IDs by weighted durable-entity fingerprint matching; finding v7 adds continues_campaign/windows_observed. W15 streaming next; W17 Rust bridge will need the user's machine
